syntax_3413.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3413.title: WebSocket Server Template
syntax_3413.description:
Not an actual event, but rather a template for a websocket server, with the specified ID. Under the main "event" line you can have seven different sub-scopes that handle websocket events:
on start: This is called when the websocket server is started.
on stop: This is called when the websocket server is stopped. 
This is not called if the websocket server is stopped due to the Minecraft server stopping.
on handshake: This is called before 'on open', when a client has sent a request handshake, allowing you to modify the response handshake to be sent as well as verify that the client's request is valid, and refuse the request if you deem it to be invalid. Note that you can't send messages to the client at this point.
on open: This is called when a client opens a websocket connection with this websocket server.
on message: This is called when the other end of a websocket connection sends a message.
on error: This is called when an error occurs related to a websocket connection.If this section is not present and the config option 'print_websocket_errors' is true, then WebSKT will print the error to the console.
on close: This is called when a websocket connection is closed.
There are also two more sub-scopes that control aspects other than events: 
server var[iable]s: Under this sub-scope you can list variable names that should be scoped at the level of the websocket server instance, meaning that the values of those variables are maintained through all different events.
websocket var[iable]s: Under this sub-scope you can list variable names that should be scoped at the level of the particular connection to a websocket client, meaning that the values of those variables are maintained for events involving the same particular client, but will have separate values for events involving other clients or events not associated with particular clients.

en:
Not an actual event, but rather a template for a websocket server, with the specified ID. Under the main "event" line you can have seven different sub-scopes that handle websocket events:
on start: This is called when the websocket server is started.
on stop: This is called when the websocket server is stopped. 
This is not called if the websocket server is stopped due to the Minecraft server stopping.
on handshake: This is called before 'on open', when a client has sent a request handshake, allowing you to modify the response handshake to be sent as well as verify that the client's request is valid, and refuse the request if you deem it to be invalid. Note that you can't send messages to the client at this point.
on open: This is called when a client opens a websocket connection with this websocket server.
on message: This is called when the other end of a websocket connection sends a message.
on error: This is called when an error occurs related to a websocket connection.If this section is not present and the config option 'print_websocket_errors' is true, then WebSKT will print the error to the console.
on close: This is called when a websocket connection is closed.
There are also two more sub-scopes that control aspects other than events: 
server var[iable]s: Under this sub-scope you can list variable names that should be scoped at the level of the websocket server instance, meaning that the values of those variables are maintained through all different events.
websocket var[iable]s: Under this sub-scope you can list variable names that should be scoped at the level of the particular connection to a websocket client, meaning that the values of those variables are maintained for events involving the same particular client, but will have separate values for events involving other clients or events not associated with particular clients.

syntax_3414.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3414.title: Request is Accepted
syntax_3414.description: Used in the 'on handshake' section of a websocket server template. Checks whether the client's request was accepted or refused. Can be set.

en: Used in the 'on handshake' section of a websocket server template. Checks whether the client's request was accepted or refused. Can be set.

syntax_3417.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3417.title: Stop WebSocket Server
syntax_3417.description: Stops the WebSocket server at the specified port, optionally specifying a timeout in milliseconds.
syntax_3417.syntax_pattern: stop [the] websocket server at port %number% [with timeout %-number%]

en: Stops the WebSocket server at the specified port, optionally specifying a timeout in milliseconds.

syntax_3418.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3418.title: WebSocket Send
syntax_3418.description: Sends the specified messages through the specified websockets. If no websockets are specified, the messages are sent through the event-websocket.

en: Sends the specified messages through the specified websockets. If no websockets are specified, the messages are sent through the event-websocket.

syntax_3420.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3420.title: Content of Handshake
syntax_3420.description: An expression for the content (a byte array) stored in the specified handshake.

en: An expression for the content (a byte array) stored in the specified handshake.

syntax_3421.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3421.title: Connection State of WebSocket
syntax_3421.description: An expression for the connection state of the specified websocket.

en: An expression for the connection state of the specified websocket.

syntax_3422.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3422.title: HTTP Header Names of Handshake
syntax_3422.description: An expression for a list of the names of the HTTP headers of the specified handshake.

en: An expression for a list of the names of the HTTP headers of the specified handshake.

syntax_3423.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3423.title: HTTP Header of Handshake
syntax_3423.description: An expression for the value of the HTTP header with the specified name of the specified handshake.

en: An expression for the value of the HTTP header with the specified name of the specified handshake.

syntax_3424.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3424.title: Host of WebSocket
syntax_3424.description: An expression for the host, local or external, of the specified websocket.
syntax_3424.syntax_pattern:
[the] local host of %websocket%
%websocket%'[s] local host
[the] remote host of %websocket%
%websocket%'[s] remote host
[the] external host of %websocket%
%websocket%'[s] external host

en: An expression for the host, local or external, of the specified websocket.

syntax_3426.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3426.title: HTTP Status Message of Handshake
syntax_3426.description: An expression for the HTTP status message of the specified handshake. This can only exist in handshakes sent by the server (ex. 'response' - see the Handshake Request/Response/New expression).
syntax_3426.syntax_pattern:
[the] http status message of %handshake%
%handshake%'[s] http status message
[the] handshake http status message of %handshake%
%handshake%'[s] handshake http status message

en: An expression for the HTTP status message of the specified handshake. This can only exist in handshakes sent by the server (ex. 'response' - see the Handshake Request/Response/New expression).

syntax_3427.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3427.title: HTTP Status of Handshake
syntax_3427.description: An expression for the HTTP status of the specified handshake. This can only exist in handshakes sent by the server (ex. 'response' - see the Handshake Request/Response/New expression).
syntax_3427.syntax_pattern:
[the] http status of %handshake%
%handshake%'[s] http status
[the] handshake http status of %handshake%
%handshake%'[s] handshake http status

en: An expression for the HTTP status of the specified handshake. This can only exist in handshakes sent by the server (ex. 'response' - see the Handshake Request/Response/New expression).

syntax_3428.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3428.title: ID of WebSocket Client
syntax_3428.description: An expression for the ID of the websocket client template controlling the specified websocket. This will not be set if the specified websocket belongs to a websocket server (meaning the connection was initiated externally).

en: An expression for the ID of the websocket client template controlling the specified websocket. This will not be set if the specified websocket belongs to a websocket server (meaning the connection was initiated externally).

syntax_3429.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3429.title: ID of WebSocket Server
syntax_3429.description: An expression for the ID of the websocket server template controlling the websocket server at the specified port.
syntax_3429.syntax_pattern: [the] id of [the] websocket server at port %number%

en: An expression for the ID of the websocket server template controlling the websocket server at the specified port.

syntax_3430.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3430.title: New WebSocket
syntax_3430.description: Creates a new websocket connection using the websocket client with the specified id, connecting to the specified URI. Optionally, you can specify additional HTTP headers, which you can use to add additional information in the initial connection (ex. a password). A header is a mapping from one string (the name) to another (the value). Each header has a unique name.You can specify headers by creating a new handshake, setting its headers, and then specifying it in the syntax here. Keep in mind that any other information stored in the handshake will be ignored by this expression.
syntax_3430.syntax_pattern: [[a] new] websocket %string% connected to uri %string% [with (handshake|http) headers %-handshake%]

en: Creates a new websocket connection using the websocket client with the specified id, connecting to the specified URI. Optionally, you can specify additional HTTP headers, which you can use to add additional information in the initial connection (ex. a password). A header is a mapping from one string (the name) to another (the value). Each header has a unique name.You can specify headers by creating a new handshake, setting its headers, and then specifying it in the syntax here. Keep in mind that any other information stored in the handshake will be ignored by this expression.

syntax_3431.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3431.title: Port of WebSocket
syntax_3431.description: An expression for the port, local or external, of the specified websocket.
syntax_3431.syntax_pattern:
[the] local port of %websocket%
%websocket%'[s] local port
[the] remote port of %websocket%
%websocket%'[s] remote port
[the] external port of %websocket%
%websocket%'[s] external port

en: An expression for the port, local or external, of the specified websocket.

syntax_3432.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3432.title: Resource Descriptor of Handshake
syntax_3432.description: An expression for the resource descriptor of the specified handshake. This can only exist in handshakes sent by the client (ex. 'request' - see the Handshake Request/Response/New expression).
syntax_3432.syntax_pattern:
[the] resource descriptor of %handshake%
%handshake%'[s] resource descriptor
[the] handshake resource descriptor of %handshake%
%handshake%'[s] handshake resource descriptor

en: An expression for the resource descriptor of the specified handshake. This can only exist in handshakes sent by the client (ex. 'request' - see the Handshake Request/Response/New expression).

syntax_3433.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3433.title: WebSocket Server Port
syntax_3433.description: For use under 'websocket server %string%': An expression for the port on which this websocket server is open.

en: For use under 'websocket server %string%': An expression for the port on which this websocket server is open.

syntax_3434.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3434.title: Handshake
syntax_3434.description: A handshake, sent by two websockets to each other when they are initially connected. Handshakes contain information necessary to initiate a websocket connection and you can add more information (ex. a password) that you want to be sent on the initial connection.

en: A handshake, sent by two websockets to each other when they are initially connected. Handshakes contain information necessary to initiate a websocket connection and you can add more information (ex. a password) that you want to be sent on the initial connection.

syntax_3435.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3435.title: WebSocket State
syntax_3435.description: A state that a websocket connection can be in.
syntax_3435.type_usage: not yet connected closed closing open connecting not_yet_connected

en: A state that a websocket connection can be in.

syntax_3412.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3412.title: WebSocket Client Template
syntax_3412.description:
Not an actual event, but rather a template for a websocket client, with the specified ID. Under the main "event" line you can have five different sub-scopes that handle websocket events:
on open: This is called when the websocket connection initially opens.
on handshake: This is called before 'on open', when the response handshake has been received from the server, but before the websocket is technically open, meaning you can't yet send messages to the server.
on message: This is called when the other end of the websocket connection sends a message.
on error: This is called when an error occurs related to the websocket connection. If this section is not present and the config option 'print_websocket_errors' is true, then WebSKT will print the error to the console.
on close: This is called when the websocket connection is closed.
There are also two more sub-scopes that control aspects other than events: 
websocket var[iable]s: Under this sub-scope you can list variable names that should be scoped at the level of the websocket client instance, meaning that the values of those variables are maintained through different events.
constructor %string%: You can use this sub-scope to specify an alternate syntax for creating an instance of this websocket client template. The code that you write under this sub-scope will be run when that syntax is called to create a new instance. In this code you need to make sure to set the Remote Server URI expression.

en:
Not an actual event, but rather a template for a websocket client, with the specified ID. Under the main "event" line you can have five different sub-scopes that handle websocket events:
on open: This is called when the websocket connection initially opens.
on handshake: This is called before 'on open', when the response handshake has been received from the server, but before the websocket is technically open, meaning you can't yet send messages to the server.
on message: This is called when the other end of the websocket connection sends a message.
on error: This is called when an error occurs related to the websocket connection. If this section is not present and the config option 'print_websocket_errors' is true, then WebSKT will print the error to the console.
on close: This is called when the websocket connection is closed.
There are also two more sub-scopes that control aspects other than events: 
websocket var[iable]s: Under this sub-scope you can list variable names that should be scoped at the level of the websocket client instance, meaning that the values of those variables are maintained through different events.
constructor %string%: You can use this sub-scope to specify an alternate syntax for creating an instance of this websocket client template. The code that you write under this sub-scope will be run when that syntax is called to create a new instance. In this code you need to make sure to set the Remote Server URI expression.

syntax_3415.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3415.title: Close WebSocket
syntax_3415.description: Closes the specified websocket connection, optionally specifying a closing message to send.

en: Closes the specified websocket connection, optionally specifying a closing message to send.

syntax_3416.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3416.title: Start WebSocket Server
syntax_3416.description: Starts a WebSocket server using the specified server template at the specified port. A WebSocket server allows other servers/online services to initiate websocket connections with the server.

en: Starts a WebSocket server using the specified server template at the specified port. A WebSocket server allows other servers/online services to initiate websocket connections with the server.

syntax_3419.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3419.title: All WebSockets of Server
syntax_3419.description: An expression for all of the websocket connections of the websocket server at the specified port. When used under 'websocket server %string%', the port is optional, in which case it will return the websockets for the websocket server being controlled.
syntax_3419.syntax_pattern: all [[of] the] websockets [of [the] server at port %-number%]

en: An expression for all of the websocket connections of the websocket server at the specified port. When used under 'websocket server %string%', the port is optional, in which case it will return the websockets for the websocket server being controlled.

syntax_3425.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3425.title: Handshake Request/Response/New
syntax_3425.description:
An expression for some handshake:
request: The handshake sent by a websocket client to a websocket server, used in the 'on open' section of a websocket server template or the 'on handshake' section of a websocket client or server template
response: The handshake sent by a websocket server responding to a request by a websocket client, used in the 'on open' section of a websocket client template or the 'on handshake' section of a websocket client or server template
new: A new handshake object, currently only useful for specifying additional HTTP headers in the New Websocket expression

en:
An expression for some handshake:
request: The handshake sent by a websocket client to a websocket server, used in the 'on open' section of a websocket server template or the 'on handshake' section of a websocket client or server template
response: The handshake sent by a websocket server responding to a request by a websocket client, used in the 'on open' section of a websocket client template or the 'on handshake' section of a websocket client or server template
new: A new handshake object, currently only useful for specifying additional HTTP headers in the New Websocket expression

syntax_3437.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3437.title: Remote Server URI
syntax_3437.description: Used in a constructor of a websocket client template. This is the uri of the websocket server that this client instance will connect to following the execution of the constructor code. This expression should always be set in the constructor code. If it is not set, the websocket client will have no server to connect to.

en: Used in a constructor of a websocket client template. This is the uri of the websocket server that this client instance will connect to following the execution of the constructor code. This expression should always be set in the constructor code. If it is not set, the websocket client will have no server to connect to.

syntax_3436.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3436.title: WebSocket
syntax_3436.description: A websocket object representing one end of a WebSocket connection that can be used to transmit informations between multiple servers and other online services.

en: A websocket object representing one end of a WebSocket connection that can be used to transmit informations between multiple servers and other online services.
